source: MIXED measured/estimated (M3b Phase 2). LOCAL side: `gemma-4-31b-dense` is a real wall-meter MEASUREMENT (PM 231 E @ 96 W steady decode on the Strix Halo / Radeon 8060S gfx1151 via llama.cpp Vulkan, WSL2 interop, 2026-06-25); `gemma-4-26b-a4b` + `gemma-4-12b-unified` remain deterministic `costroid bench` ESTIMATES (no hardware) over the bundled Gemma 4 manifest (crates/costroid-power/models/gemma4.v1.json, as_of 2026-06-20) + the dated hardware/electricity profile (crates/costroid-power/profiles/hardware.v1.json, strix-halo-128gb@2026-06-20). CLOUD side: the M2 layered cloud pricing catalog (curated@2026-06-02, costroid-core) priced per token; the pure costroid-core::breakeven crossover for the outcome. 26b/12b tok/s are community-analog ESTIMATES (gemma4.v1.json); 31b tok/s is MEASURED. Quality is as-published (cited, never re-derived, R10).
note: MIXED measured/estimated (M3b Phase 2, 2026-06-25). `gemma-4-31b-dense` is a real captured wall-meter run (`measurement_mode` = `"measured_wallmeter"`, llama.cpp Vulkan): its energy/cost/tok-s are MEASURED, over the run's real 18,018-token basis (an ~18-token benchmark prompt + 18,000 generated). `gemma-4-26b-a4b` + `gemma-4-12b-unified` stay "estimated — pending M3b measurement" (R8/R10): derived from a profile + a community-analog throughput estimate, NOT a captured joules/token. The break-even keeps the comparable 2,000-in/18,000-out (20,000-token) workload + the unchanged cloud list prices (POST-M3B-REFRESH Step 3); only the local power moved to measured for 31b — so the 31b run's per-token figure is over its real 18,018 basis while its break-even `e` is over the 20,000 workload. Money is exact decimal strings, never f64. Cloud costs are COUNTERFACTUAL list-price estimates from the catalog (your tokens × current list prices), never an actual cloud bill. Costroid ships NO weights (user-downloaded GGUF). The 26b/12b raw rows regenerate byte-for-byte with `SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1781913600 costroid bench`; the 31b raw row is a captured measurement pinned by its `.sha256` (see README.md). Pinned to Gemma 4 (Apache-2.0).

cloud_pricing.note: Per-meter list prices (USD per 1M tokens) resolved from the bundled layered catalog — the cloud side is a counterfactual list-price estimate, never an actual cloud bill.
